note: seq=56 (ASYNC CANARY RE-DIAGNOSE -- reliable publish). seq-55's gate ran (heartbeat last_seq=55) but BOTH its status pushes failed (transient gh_put), so the selftest error + box python version were lost. This re-fetches the gate (now with a 5x gh_put retry incl. 409-sha-refetch) and re-runs it: re-runs the async --selftest capturing the box python version + full output, publishes status/hetzner/async-canary.json reliably, and starts atlas-enrich-async@1 only if selftest passes. Always exits 0. Read-only except the conditional canary start. Allowlist-safe.

steps.note: seq=56: re-diagnose async canary with retrying publish.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/async_canary_gate.py':'f43133cc34fe1387d9450a65e935bb2372436ba6a98fe6b107a8d8574f380503'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"
